title: Everlasting Love
wikitext:
[]: Everlasting Love             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sun Nov 13, 2005 4:41 pm Does anyone think that they will love the shoe Neon Genesis Evangelion forever. Also does anyone believe that that will love one character from that show forever? I know that I will always love Evangelion I know that i will always love Kaworu for eternity, since after all we are soul mates. we were ment to be together for ever. Love always Yuko-chan

[]: Re: Everlasting Love             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Sun Nov 13, 2005 6:27 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: Does anyone think that they will love the shoe Neon Genesis Evangelion forever. Also does anyone believe that that will love one character from that show forever? I know that I will always love Evangelion I know that i will always love Kaworu for eternity, since after all we are soul mates. we were ment to be together for ever. Love always Yuko-chan

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Sun Nov 13, 2005 6:48 pm Woah... Yeah, that pretty much sums it all up. This is the first time I've seen someone with this level of obssesion.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Nov 13, 2005 6:50 pm I've largy moved on, but lack of money has kept me from finding a new favorate series. That and unempoyment has had me hanging out here more often :)

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sun Nov 13, 2005 7:17 pm You know, have obsession issues, but I know it sure as hell wont last forever. 5 years, if I'm lucky

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Sun Nov 13, 2005 8:21 pm Do you mean love as in being in love? My little period of "obsession" (if you with to call it that) ended a long time ago. But it will always live on in a special place inside of my heart, and I will watch it every now and then.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Nov 13, 2005 8:44 pm I'll probably remember and like Evangelion for life, because it does sort of...alter your outlook on things. But I won't be obsessed with it. My period of Eva obsession is slowly fading out. It's lasted for about 3.5 years, so it's about time.. o__o;; Buuut, I still watch it a lot, and it's still my favorite series. ...though I feel sorry for you if you're destined to be with a fictional character for eternity. That's gotta be pretty lonely.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Nov 13, 2005 10:32 pm So is this part of the wave of new people from Adult Swim we've been anticipating?

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sun Nov 13, 2005 10:46 pm drinian wrote: So is this part of the wave of new people from Adult Swim we've been anticipating?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 13, 2005 11:05 pm I'm hoping to move on from Eva. I think I'll always like/love/appreciate it to some degree. I'm hoping to culminate my Eva fandom with a book and be down with Eva for a while. It does tire me out after so long.

[]:             Quote Post by Xanatose » Mon Nov 14, 2005 12:53 am I think I moved on a few months after watching the last of the series episodes, but fell back into liking it after I read Eva:R. Funny story about that, but I'll sum up to say that I read Eva:R before I got to see EOE, so you can imagine the way it altered my outlook on things. But even after that, I got over Eva again about a year later. Today however I am involved in writing Evangelion fanfiction as an exercise whenever I get writer's block. It tends to help, so it's never too far from my mind, but mostly only because I have to think of it in terms of how I can change it, which doesn't do much for my level of satisfaction. Suffice it to say, I'm utterly disapointed in how Evangelion turned out, but respect the original creators for their ambition. I always hope there will be something new added to the franchise, but then they go and make the various Raising projects, Iron Maiden games, and Amanda Win Lee says there is no "I" in Japanese, and I feel good about not caring as much anymore (which is not to say I don't care enough for it to bother me, and that's unfortunate.) Oh well, Kaworu. We'll always have our song. *listens to the 9th symphany and swoons*

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Nov 14, 2005 2:18 am Heidroch wrote: drinian wrote: So is this part of the wave of new people from Adult Swim we've been anticipating?

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Mon Nov 14, 2005 3:07 am Carl Horn wrote: Well, isn't that true?

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Mon Nov 14, 2005 4:48 am I probably will always remember it...

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Mon Nov 14, 2005 7:25 am I WILL LOVE EVANGELION FOR EVAAAAAAAAAAAAA(-01)!!!! I will show it to my childs, and my childs to their childs and blah blah blah blah blah blah........

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Mon Nov 14, 2005 3:07 pm Six Winged Angel wrote: I WILL LOVE EVANGELION FOR EVAAAAAAAAAAAAA(-01)!!!! I will show it to my childs, and my childs to their childs and blah blah blah blah blah blah........

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Mon Nov 14, 2005 3:24 pm dude please :P

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Nov 14, 2005 5:12 pm I'll finish with Evangelion once the pieces have all fitted together to my satisfaction. Then I shall sleep, my mind no longer tormented with question and doubt, forever at rest knowing Eva's true mysteries, or content to let them lie undisclosed.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Nov 14, 2005 6:35 pm Heidroch wrote: Carl Horn wrote: Well, isn't that true?

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Wed Nov 16, 2005 3:28 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'll finish with Evangelion once the pieces have all fitted together to my satisfaction. Then I shall sleep, my mind no longer tormented with question and doubt, forever at rest knowing Eva's true mysteries, or content to let them lie undisclosed.
